perturbation.original_text: 11 Notices 11.1 A notice or other communication under or in connection with th is Agreement shall be in writing, in Eng lish and shall be delive red personally or sent by fax, to the party due to receive the no tice or communication at its address set out in this Agreement or another address as specified by that party by wr itten notice to the other. 11.2 In the absence of evidence of earlier r eceipt, any notice or other communication shall be deemed to ha ve been duly given, if delivered personally , when left at the address referred to in Clause 0 and if sent by fax, on completion of its transmission.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.1 Each party undertakes within three business days of the receipt of a written request of the other party or on termination of this Agreement, at the option of the other party: (a) to return to the other party all of the other party's Confidential Information and that part of all documents and other material in its po ssession, custody or control that bear or incorporate any pa rt of the other pa rty's Confidential Information; or (b) to destroy by shredding or incineration all of the other pa rty's Confidential Information and that part of all documents and other material in its possession, custody or control which bear or inco rporate any part of the other party's Confidential Information and take reason able steps to expung e all Confidential Information (and any copy that may have been ma de) from any computer, word processor or other device containing the Confidential Information. Any destruction of Confidential Information will be certified in writing to the other party by an authorised officer supervising it. 11 Notices 11.1 A notice or other communication under or in connection with th is Agreement shall be in writing, in Eng lish and shall be delive red personally or sent by fax, to the party due to receive the no tice or communication at its address set out in this Agreement or another address as specified by that party by wr itten notice to the other. 11.2 In the absence of evidence of earlier r eceipt, any notice or other communication shall be deemed to ha ve been duly given, if delivered personally , when left at the address referred to in Clause 0 and if sent by fax, on completion of its transmission.
perturbation.explanation: The clause specifying how notices are to be delivered is moved to the section on Returning or Destroying confidential information. Proper notice is fundamental for legal proceedings, and obscuring it like this hinders due process.
perturbation.law_explanation: The Civil Procedure Rules mandate proper service of documents. By misplacing the notice clause within the clause about returning documentation, the contract deviates from standard legal practices for providing adequate notice, making compliance with procedural rules more challenging.
perturbation.justification: The original text has been changed by moving the clause specifying how notices are to be delivered is moved to the section on Returning or Destroying confidential information. This constitutes a structural flaw. The Law Explanation states that the Civil Procedure Rules mandate proper service of documents. By misplacing the notice clause within the clause about returning documentation, the contract deviates from standard legal practices for providing adequate notice, making compliance with procedural rules more challenging. As a result, the modified text contradicts the law by obscuring the method for proper notice, a key element for legal proceedings and due process.
